what: lssdoc: Word codebook documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
title: lssdoc: Word codebook documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
text: lssdoc turns LimeSurvey .lss export polished Word (.docx) codebook document ethics committees, survey methodologists, translators. renders questionnaire content side side four languages, runs automated integrity audit, produces layout reads published codebook – developer dump. Two output templates: "cards" – one detached block per item (meta table + question table), stacked vertically. Closest printed questionnaire respondent see.  "table" – one dense codebook table covering whole document: every variable one tinted Question row carrying metadata, followed one white Value rows holding answer codes labels per language. Group banners, welcome text, description end text become labelled rows table codebook reads single artifact.  things lssdoc takes care : Multilingual chrome. Column headers, row labels, type labels (Single choice / Multiple choice / Text / Number …), Value descriptors, audit section – everything outside survey content – translated English, French, German, Spanish Italian. Independent survey’s content languages, can keep FR/DE questionnaire switch document scaffolding English (vice versa) single argument. Variable-centric compound rendering. Array, multiple-choice, multiple-numerical dual-scale array questions documented per subquestion, real variable code (parent_subq) meta row – parent code never appears data export. Methodological type labels. UI distinctions like “List (radio)” vs “List (dropdown)” collapse “Single choice”, response semantics identical actual codes appear Value section. Type cell reads methodologist’s vocabulary, LimeSurvey’s. Automated audit. Missing translations, unreachable filter expressions, dangling references, structural inconsistencies – flagged inline rendered item , ask , dedicated section. AI; rule-based reviewable. Local-processing. network calls, third-party services. questionnaire content never leaves machine R runs.

title: lssdoc: Word codebook documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
text: development version, GitHub, pak (recommended): remotes: package depends officer flextable (declared Suggests – parse audit paths work without ).

title: lssdoc: Word codebook documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
text: output uses "cards" template default, chrome language matching survey’s primary content language. Open review.docx Word, click Yes prompted update fields (page numbers table contents).
code: library(lssdoc)  # Parse the .lss and render the Word document in a single call. lss_to_docx("survey.lss", "review.docx")

title: lssdoc: Word codebook documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
text: languages controls content columns appear order. chrome_lang independent: pass "en" keep English-labeled scaffolding French German content side side.

title: lssdoc: Word codebook documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
text: cover page picks authors block (clickable ORCID hyperlinks), description (URLs auto-detected made clickable), privacy / admin settings rows metadata table.
code: lss_to_docx(   "survey.lss",   "ethics_review.docx",   languages              = c("fr", "de"),   template               = "table",   chrome_lang            = "fr",   show_privacy_settings  = TRUE,   # anonymized / save partial / IP / referrer / timestamp   show_admin_settings    = TRUE,   # alias / end URL / active   authors = list(     list(name        = "Amal Tawfik",          affiliation = "HES-SO Valais-Wallis",          orcid       = "0009-0006-2422-1555")   ),   description = paste0(     "Validated as part of the SNSF project XYZ. ",     "Reference: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.123456"   ) )

title: lssdoc: Word codebook documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
text: Pure data: one row per variable header, one row per value code, nothing else.
code: lss_to_docx(   "survey.lss",   "minimal.docx",   template         = "table",   show_welcome     = FALSE,   show_endtext     = FALSE,   show_description = FALSE,   show_groups      = FALSE,   show_toc         = FALSE,   show_audit       = FALSE,   show_index       = FALSE )

title: lssdoc: Word codebook documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
code: # Parse once, audit and render separately. lss <- parse_lss("survey.lss")  audit <- audit_lss(lss) print(audit)  # console summary of flagged anomalies  render_lss_docx(   lss,   output     = "review.docx",   languages  = c("fr", "de"),   template   = "table",   show_audit = TRUE )

title: lssdoc: Word codebook documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
code: # Same arguments as lss_to_docx() -- writes the .docx then converts # it to .pdf locally via LibreOffice (must be installed and on PATH). lss_to_pdf("survey.lss", "review.pdf", template = "table")

title: lssdoc: Word codebook documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
text: rendered .docx uses editorial petrol-blue palette tuned publication-grade output (#133B52 primary, #3A7C8C accent, #E9F2F6 #F4F8FA tinted bands, #D3DCE2 soft grid). Calibri body / Consolas monospace default; font font_code arguments accept string override.

what: Audit a parsed LimeSurvey structure for reviewable anomalies — audit_lss
title: Audit a parsed LimeSurvey structure for reviewable anomalies — audit_lss
text: Inspect lss object flag anomalies can detected without AI. audit meant guide human reviewer, silently correct anything: every finding names precise location severity.

title: Audit a parsed LimeSurvey structure for reviewable anomalies — audit_lss
Audit a parsed LimeSurvey structure for reviewable anomalies — audit_lss
text: lss lss object returned parse_lss().

title: Audit a parsed LimeSurvey structure for reviewable anomalies — audit_lss
text: object class lss_audit: list file, languages, summary counts, findings data frame (severity, check, location, language, message). print() method .data.frame() method.

title: Audit a parsed LimeSurvey structure for reviewable anomalies — audit_lss
text: Checks performed: Missing translations – question, help, answer, subquestion text exists least one language empty another. Empty languages – translatable text empty every language. Duplicate codes – question variable code repeated survey, answer/subquestion code repeated within one question. Missing options type – question whose type requires answer options subquestions none (per type taxonomy). Orphan references – subquestion answer points question exist.

title: Audit a parsed LimeSurvey structure for reviewable anomalies — audit_lss
code: lss <- parse_lss(system.file("extdata", "hesav_2026.lss",   package = "lssdoc" )) audit <- audit_lss(lss) print(audit) #>  #> ── lssdoc audit ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> File: /tmp/RtmpB56o68/temp_libpath1a35384f8905/lssdoc/extdata/hesav_2026.lss #> Languages: "de" and "fr" #> ✔ No anomalies detected.

text: Use LibreOffice (Word, Windows) headless mode convert generated .docx PDF. Conversion stays user's machine: upload, network call. LibreOffice (soffice executable) must available; function reports actionable error .

title: Convert a .docx to .pdf locally — lss_docx_to_pdf
text: LibreOffice headless refresh Word field values (TOC, page counts) conversion, even document flagged update fields open. result, table--contents field produced render_lss_docx() appears empty converted PDF. obtain PDF populated TOC, open generated .docx Word – TOC refreshes automatically – use File > Save > PDF. Working directly .docx simplest path.

title: Convert a .docx to .pdf locally — lss_docx_to_pdf
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ lss <- parse_lss(system.file("extdata", "hesav_2026.lss",   package = "lssdoc" )) tmp_docx <- tempfile(fileext = ".docx") render_lss_docx(lss, tmp_docx) lss_docx_to_pdf(tmp_docx, "rapport.pdf") } # }

what: lssdoc: Generate Word Review Documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' Files — lssdoc-package
title: lssdoc: Generate Word Review Documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' Files — lssdoc-package
text: Build professional Word ('.docx') review document LimeSurvey '.lss' export, displaying four languages side side. output supports human review multilingual questionnaires ethics committees, translators, methodologists: reproduces questionnaire content faithfully flags automatically detectable anomalies require AI. Processing fully local, questionnaire uploaded third-party service.

title: lssdoc: Generate Word Review Documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' Files — lssdoc-package
text: Maintainer: Amal Tawfik amal.tawfik@hesav.ch (ORCID) (ROR) [copyright holder] Authors: Amal Tawfik amal.tawfik@hesav.ch (ORCID) (ROR) [copyright holder]

title: Parse a LimeSurvey .lss file — parse_lss
text: Read LimeSurvey survey structure export (.lss, XML file) turn structured lss object rest package can audit render. Parsing fully local: file never uploaded anywhere.

title: Parse a LimeSurvey .lss file — parse_lss
Parse a LimeSurvey .lss file — parse_lss
text: path Path .lss file.

title: Parse a LimeSurvey .lss file — parse_lss
text: object class lss: list holding survey languages metadata plus one data frame per .lss section. Structural sections (surveys, groups, questions, subquestions, answers, question_attributes, conditions) kept separate localized text sections (survey_language_settings, group_l10ns, question_l10ns, answer_l10ns), carry per-language titles, labels, help texts. values read verbatim character.

title: Parse a LimeSurvey .lss file — parse_lss
text: .lss format LimeSurvey XML export. Since DBVersion 4xx/7xx translatable text lives dedicated localization sections (*_l10ns), keyed language, structural sections hold identifiers settings. parse_lss() reads every section tidy data frame without mutating user-facing identifier text. field present empty (e.g. <help/>) read ""; field absent row read NA.

title: Parse a LimeSurvey .lss file — parse_lss

what: Render the audit alone as a focused Word document — render_lss_audit_docx
title: Render the audit alone as a focused Word document — render_lss_audit_docx
text: Build short, action-oriented .docx contains audit findings: cover page full review document, summary counts, one table per severity (errors, warnings, notes) listing every finding location message. Use QA follow-share issues colleague without distributing full questionnaire.

title: Render the audit alone as a focused Word document — render_lss_audit_docx
code: render_lss_audit_docx(   lss,   output,   languages = NULL,   logo = NULL,   logo_width = 1.5,   logo_height = 0.75,   font = NULL,   font_code = NULL,   colors = NULL,   authors = NULL,   description = NULL,   chrome_lang = NULL )

title: Render the audit alone as a focused Word document — render_lss_audit_docx
text: lss lss object returned parse_lss(). output Path .docx file create. languages Character vector language codes cover page. Defaults languages survey. logo Optional path PNG JPEG image display top cover page. NULL (default) keeps cover logo-free. logo_width, logo_height Image dimensions inches. Defaults tuned 2:1 logo (1.5 x 0.75 inches). font Body font name. NULL (default) keeps Calibri. See render_lss_docx() guidance overrides. font_code Monospace font used code-like content (variable codes, raw expressions). NULL (default) keeps Consolas. colors Optional named list hex color overrides. shape accepted names render_lss_docx(). authors, description Optional cover-page credit block free-form note. shapes render_lss_docx(). chrome_lang Language used document chrome (column headers, row labels, audit section). Supported: "en", "fr", "de", "es", "". NULL (default) follows languages[1] supported, otherwise "en".

title: Render the audit alone as a focused Word document — render_lss_audit_docx
text: output path, invisibly.

title: Render the audit alone as a focused Word document — render_lss_audit_docx
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ lss <- parse_lss(system.file("extdata", "limesurvey_survey_751689.lss",   package = "lssdoc" )) render_lss_audit_docx(lss, tempfile(fileext = ".docx")) } # }

what: Render a parsed LimeSurvey structure to a Word document — render_lss_docx
title: Render a parsed LimeSurvey structure to a Word document — render_lss_docx
text: Build professional .docx review document lss object, displaying four languages side side. question becomes compact flextable meta header (variable code, type, mandatory, filter) shown , language column headers, question text per language, subquestion answer-option rows underneath – codes left, labels per language right. Headings, metadata cover page, optional table contents, optional audit summary tie document together. Rendering uses suggested packages officer flextable; must installed.

title: Render a parsed LimeSurvey structure to a Word document — render_lss_docx
code: render_lss_docx(   lss,   output,   languages = NULL,   template = c("cards", "table"),   layout = c("auto", "side-by-side", "stacked"),   show_audit = TRUE,   show_help = TRUE,   show_attrs = c("prefix", "suffix", "other_replace_text", "validation",     "exclude_all_others", "exclude_all_others_auto"),   show_technical_attrs = FALSE,   page_format = c("auto", "A4-portrait", "A4-landscape", "A3"),   show_toc = TRUE,   show_index = TRUE,   show_header_title = TRUE,   show_source = TRUE,   show_item_heading = FALSE,   show_raw_filter = TRUE,   show_groups = TRUE,   show_welcome = TRUE,   show_endtext = TRUE,   show_description = TRUE,   show_privacy_settings = FALSE,   show_admin_settings = FALSE,   title = NULL,   logo = NULL,   logo_width = 1.5,   logo_height = 0.75,   font = NULL,   font_code = NULL,   colors = NULL,   authors = NULL,   description = NULL,   chrome_lang = NULL )

title: Render a parsed LimeSurvey structure to a Word document — render_lss_docx
text: lss lss object returned parse_lss(). output Path .docx file create. languages Character vector language codes display, order appear columns. Use subset (display languages want) order ; example c("fr", "de") puts French first, c("de", "fr") puts German first. first language treated primary language: question heading shown table contents includes question text language, group headings fall back . Requesting language absent survey error (lssdoc_unknown_language). Defaults languages found .lss file, order <languages> section. template Output style. "cards" (default) renders one detached pair tables per item (meta table + item table) stacked vertically, survey's content languages displayed side--side item table. "table" renders single dense table covering whole document codebook style: every variable one row, meta columns (| Variable | Type | Mandatory | Filter) come first, one column per content language carrying question stem, subquestion label (applicable), response modalities stacked underneath. Group banners become merged section rows inside table; column header repeats every page automatically. table template auto-promotes page format A4 landscape 2+ languages (overridable page_format). layout Reserved future use. Currently "side--side" . show_audit Logical; include audit summary section near top inline markers questions carry findings. show_help Logical; include question help texts question text. show_attrs Character vector question attributes display question text present. show_technical_attrs Logical; include technical attributes answer_order location_*. page_format Page format. "auto" picks portrait one two languages landscape three. Use "A4-portrait", "A4-landscape", "A3" force layout. show_toc Logical; include table contents listing groups survey. Skipped automatically survey fewer two groups (single-group survey makes TOC redundant). Items TOC; use show_index navigable variable index. show_index Logical; append variable index end document listing every item code item number, sorted alphabetically. Useful cross-referencing specific variable. show_header_title Logical; show survey title top right every page, one line per displayed language. Long titles truncated 80 characters trailing ellipsis. Default TRUE. FALSE, X/Y page counter shows bottom right. show_source Logical; show Source file name Survey ID rows cover metadata table. Default TRUE keeps traceability; pass FALSE hide (reviewers prefer expose LimeSurvey survey id internal filename). show_item_heading Logical; show bold heading "N. variable" item. Default FALSE: meta table starts item directly, compact layout. Set TRUE add heading line scroll-time navigation; item number already present meta table's column variable index heading redundant cross-reference purposes. show_raw_filter Logical; TRUE (default) Filter cell meta table shows human-readable form top raw LimeSurvey relevance expression smaller italic gray underneath. Set FALSE cleaner cell shows plain form (raw expression still shown simplified). show_groups Logical; show group banners (cards layout) group rows (table layout). Default TRUE. Surveys sometimes use groups internal organization tools without showing respondents; pass FALSE flatten document single sequence items section breaks. show_welcome Logical; include survey's welcome text (surveyls_welcometext) document. Default TRUE. welcome text multilingual appears side--side block cards layout embedded row codebook table. show_endtext Logical; include survey's end text (surveyls_endtext). Default TRUE. multilingual treatment show_welcome. show_description Logical; include survey's description (surveyls_description). Default TRUE. description short "survey " multilingual intro LimeSurvey shows welcome text landing page; appears welcome block cards layout Description row codebook table. show_privacy_settings Logical; surface survey-level privacy / tracking settings (anonymized, save partial, datestamp, ipaddr, refurl) additional rows cover metadata table. Default FALSE reviewers need ; set TRUE ethics committee submission flags part methodological assessment. show_admin_settings Logical; surface survey-level administrative settings (alias, end URL description, active flag) additional rows cover metadata table. Default FALSE. end URL respondent redirected submission; reviewers may want check third-party redirects. title Optional override survey title shown cover page top-right header. NULL (default) uses per- language titles .lss survey settings. Pass single string use title every displayed language, named character vector keyed language code (e.g. c(fr = "Mon titre", de = "Mein Titel")) per-language overrides. logo Optional path image (PNG JPEG) display top cover page. .lss file embed logo, image must supplied caller. NULL (default) keeps cover logo-free, matching neutral style survey-methodology references (ESS, MOSAiCH, Panel). logo_width, logo_height Image dimensions inches. Defaults tuned 2:1 logo (1.5 x 0.75 inches). Resize pre-crop image fit different aspect ratio. font Body font name. NULL (default) keeps Calibri, pre-installed every recent Windows Office metric-substituted Carlito (OFL) Mac Linux LibreOffice, column widths stay stable across platforms. Pass string override (e.g. "Source Sans 3", "IBM Plex Sans", corporate brand font). string passed Word; install font machine opens document, otherwise reader's application substitute fallback face. font_code Monospace font used code-like content: variable column meta table, raw relevance expression filter cell, variable index entries. NULL (default) keeps Consolas; pass "JetBrains Mono" "IBM Plex Mono" sharper code style installed reader's machine. colors Optional named list hex color overrides editorial petrol-blue palette. NULL (default) keeps package palette intact. Accepted names: "primary" (group filets, headers text item top borders, 1.0 pt strokes), "accent" (hyperlinks, ORCID iD, URL auto-links, group -line cards layout), "band" (light header backgrounds meta table headers codebook's Question rows), "band_dark" (meta-table dark header cards layout), "zebra" (-light tint codebook Question rows table template), "grid" (0.5 pt border color used every internal table line), "text" "muted". value must hex string ("#XXXXXX" "#XXX"). Unknown keys rejected classed condition typo never silently turns -op. Useful honoring institutional brand: e.g. colors = list(primary = "#5C9F1A", accent = "#7FA82E") produces LimeSurvey-green codebook instead default petrol blue. authors Optional credit block questionnaire's designers, displayed cover page subtitle. author shown centered line Name \u2014 Affiliation; ORCID iD provided, smaller monospace line shows ORCID 0000-0000-0000-0000 hyperlink https://orcid.org/<id>. Accepts: NULL (default): authorship block. unnamed character vector (c("Amal Tawfik", "John Doe")): entry becomes line affiliation. named character vector (c("Amal Tawfik" = "HES-Valais")): names authors, values affiliations. Use empty string "" render author without affiliation. list named lists full form, e.g. list(list(name = "Amal Tawfik", affiliation = "HES-Valais", orcid = "0009-0006-2422-1555"), list(name = "John Doe", affiliation = "UNIL")). name field required; affiliation orcid fields optional. description Optional free-form text shown cover page authors block. Useful citation hint, funding acknowledgement, methodology note, link related publication. Pass single string; line breaks (\n) split block separate centered lines. http:// https:// token rendered clickable hyperlink, DOI URLs article permalinks become navigable. Plain text otherwise. chrome_lang Language used chrome document (column headers, row labels, navigation titles, MOSAiCH-style type labels, Value descriptors, audit section). Independent languages, controls survey's content columns. Supported values: "en", "fr", "de", "es", "". NULL (default) follows languages[1] supported chrome language, otherwise falls back "en". Pass value explicitly force specific chrome language regardless content – e.g. chrome_lang = "en" languages = c("fr", "de") produces English-labeled document French German survey content. Spanish Italian translations reviewed native speaker publishing official document.

title: Render a parsed LimeSurvey structure to a Word document — render_lss_docx
text: output path, invisibly.

what: "LimeSurvey last save" date on the cover
title: Render a parsed LimeSurvey structure to a Word document — render_lss_docx
text: cover metadata table carries row labelled "LimeSurvey last save" (localized equivalent). read verbatim surveys.lastmodified column .lss, timestamp LimeSurvey writes export – table (questions, question_l10ns, answer_l10ns, groups, etc.) carries per-row modification date. row named "last save" rather "last modified" LimeSurvey bumps field reliably user clicks Save survey-level form (Settings tab); editing question text, answer label, translation Question Editor consistently update across LimeSurvey versions. date looks stale relative recent edits, workaround open Survey settings LimeSurvey, click Save (change needed), re-export .lss. next render show bumped timestamp.

title: Render a parsed LimeSurvey structure to a Word document — render_lss_docx
text: Opening rendered .docx Microsoft Word may surface security-style prompt: "document contains fields may refer files. want update fields document?". expected: package marks page-number bookmark-reference fields needing refresh footer shows correct page count table contents links resolve right pages first open (also makes headless PDF conversion via LibreOffice produce correctly paginated output without manual F9). Clicking Yes safe – document INCLUDETEXT, INCLUDEPICTURE-linked, DDE fields; external links ORCID DOI URLs cover credits, static HYPERLINK targets fetched update.

title: Render a parsed LimeSurvey structure to a Word document — render_lss_docx
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ lss <- parse_lss(system.file("extdata", "hesav_2026.lss",   package = "lssdoc" )) render_lss_docx(lss, tempfile(fileext = ".docx")) } # }

title: lssdoc 0.0.0.9000
text: survey title now appears top-right header every page (one line per displayed language, truncated 80 characters trailing ellipsis longer), replacing previous footer-left position. footer now holds compact X/Y page counter (spaces) right-aligned. Controlled renamed show_header_title argument (show_footer_title). New title argument render_lss_docx(). Pass single string override every language title, named character vector like c(fr = "Mon titre", de = "Mein Titel") per-language overrides. NULL (default) keeps per-language titles survey settings. Drives cover page header. item now renders unified mini-table left label column one row per content element (Language | Question | Help | Value 1 | Value 2 | ...). row self-describing line ESS / MOSAiCH convention. question = "Y", LimeSurvey free-text variable <parent>_other now surfaced numbered item customized other_replace_text prompt, instead buried attributes section. Table--contents entries now clickable hyperlinks jump corresponding group heading. group heading anchored bookmark TOC entry internal hyperlink anchor. Works Word, LibreOffice PDF. Group headings rendered uniformly styled paragraph (manual "N. group name" prefix Calibri 14pt blue bold) rather Word Heading 1 paragraph. Word’s Heading 1 style ships linked list definition whose number style match body font; using styled paragraph keeps whole heading typographically uniform. Trade-: groups longer appear Word’s left-side navigation pane, clickable TOC start document provides navigation every viewer. table contents now manual list group names sequential numbering, rather Word TOC field. previous field-based implementation two practical limitations: Word’s auto-refresh PAGEREF entries can fire pagination complete, producing page numbers 1 everywhere; LibreOffice refresh field values open headless PDF conversion. manual list always visible immediately every viewer (Word, LibreOffice, PDF) refresh interaction.
